title: Currency Transaction Reporting
clause: Any cash transaction exceeding $10,000 in a single business day must be reported. Multiple transactions by or on behalf of the same person totaling more than $10,000 in one day are aggregated and treated as a single transaction.

title: Structuring Prohibition
clause: Conducting transactions in amounts deliberately kept below the $10,000 reporting threshold for the purpose of evading reporting requirements is prohibited. Patterns of transactions just below the threshold are indicators of structuring and must be flagged for review.

title: High-Risk Jurisdiction Screening
clause: Transactions involving counterparties in designated high-risk jurisdictions require enhanced due diligence. New payees in such jurisdictions must be flagged for compliance review before funds are released.

title: Politically Exposed Persons
clause: Accounts and transactions associated with politically exposed persons (PEPs) - including senior government officials, heads of state or government, senior politicians, senior judicial, military, or central-bank officials, and senior executives of state-owned enterprises, whether domestic or foreign - require enhanced scrutiny and senior compliance approval. PEP status must be screened at onboarding and re-screened on material account changes.

title: Beneficial Ownership Verification
clause: For legal-entity customers, the beneficial owners holding 25 percent or more must be identified and verified. Transactions on accounts with unverified beneficial ownership above this threshold must be held for review.

title: Layered Structuring Across Accounts
clause: Sub-threshold transactions distributed across multiple accounts, branches, or instruments that aggregate to more than the reporting threshold within a short window are treated as layered structuring and must be flagged even when no single account exceeds the threshold.

title: Sanctions List Screening
clause: All counterparties must be screened against the institution's consolidated sanctions watchlist before a transaction settles. An exact name match to a listed party requires the transaction to be blocked and escalated; a near match requires manual review.

title: Suspicious Activity Escalation
clause: Activity inconsistent with a customer's known profile, source of funds, or expected transaction pattern must be escalated for suspicious-activity review. Unexplained deviations from baseline behavior are reportable indicators.

title: Cash-Intensive Business Monitoring
clause: Accounts belonging to cash-intensive businesses must be monitored against expected cash volumes for the business type. Cash deposits materially exceeding the declared business profile must be flagged for review.

title: Wire Transfer Originator Information
clause: Outbound wire transfers must carry complete and accurate originator and beneficiary information. Wires missing required originator details must be held and not released until the information is obtained.

title: Rapid Movement of Funds
clause: Funds deposited and withdrawn or transferred onward within a short period with no apparent business purpose are an indicator of pass-through or funnel activity and must be flagged for review.

title: Third-Party Funding of Accounts
clause: Deposits funded by unrelated third parties with no documented relationship to the accountholder require review. Repeated third-party funding without an explained purpose is a money-movement indicator.

title: Dormant Account Reactivation
clause: A dormant account that suddenly resumes high-value activity must be reviewed before processing. Sudden reactivation followed by large transfers is a recognized layering indicator.

title: Source of Wealth for Large Deposits
clause: Large deposits inconsistent with a customer's documented income or source of wealth require enhanced due diligence. The institution must obtain supporting documentation before the funds are made fully available.

title: Negative News Screening
clause: Customers and counterparties associated with credible adverse media relating to financial crime require enhanced review. Confirmed adverse findings must be escalated to senior compliance.

title: Correspondent Banking Due Diligence
clause: Transactions routed through correspondent or nested banking relationships require enhanced due diligence on the underlying institution. Activity from respondent banks lacking adequate controls must be flagged.

title: Trade-Based Value Discrepancy
clause: Trade transactions where invoice values are materially inconsistent with the market value of the underlying goods are an indicator of trade-based money laundering and must be flagged for review.

title: Round-Amount Transaction Patterns
clause: Repeated round-amount transactions inconsistent with ordinary commercial activity, particularly when clustered near reporting thresholds, are a structuring indicator and must be reviewed.

title: Cross-Border Cash Courier Risk
clause: Physical movement of currency or monetary instruments across borders above the declarable threshold must be reported. Undeclared cross-border movement detected by the institution must be escalated.

title: Shell Company Indicators
clause: Legal entities with no apparent operating business, no physical presence, or opaque ownership structures present elevated risk. Transactions on such accounts require enhanced verification before processing.

title: PEP Family and Close Associates
clause: Enhanced scrutiny extends to the immediate family members and known close associates of politically exposed persons. Transactions involving such parties require the same senior approval as direct PEP activity.

title: Sanctions Evasion Typologies
clause: Transactions exhibiting known sanctions-evasion typologies, including use of front companies, falsified documentation, or routing through non-sanctioned intermediaries to obscure a sanctioned ultimate party, must be blocked and escalated.

title: Customer Identification Program
clause: No account may be opened or transacted upon without verified customer identification. Transactions on accounts with incomplete identity verification must be held until identification is completed.

title: Ongoing Customer Due Diligence
clause: Customer risk profiles must be kept current. Material changes in transaction behavior, ownership, or country exposure require a refresh of due diligence and may trigger reclassification of customer risk.

title: Funnel Account Detection
clause: Accounts receiving many small deposits from disparate sources followed by consolidated withdrawals in another location are funnel-account indicators and must be flagged for suspicious-activity review.

title: Cryptocurrency Conversion Monitoring
clause: Transfers to or from virtual-asset service providers must be screened for exposure to illicit-finance typologies. Large or rapid fiat-to-virtual-asset conversions inconsistent with the customer profile must be reviewed.

title: Multiple Same-Day Branch Activity
clause: A customer conducting cash transactions at multiple branches on the same day in amounts that aggregate above the reporting threshold must have those transactions aggregated and reported, and the pattern reviewed for structuring intent.

title: Monetary Instrument Purchases
clause: Purchases of monetary instruments such as cashier's checks or money orders with cash in amounts between $3,000 and $10,000 require recordkeeping. Patterns of such purchases just below $10,000 are structuring indicators.

title: Beneficial Owner Change Monitoring
clause: A change in the beneficial ownership of a legal-entity customer requires re-verification of the new owners. Transactions following an unverified ownership change must be held for review.

title: Unusual Wire to High-Risk Geography
clause: Outbound wires to high-risk geographies that are inconsistent with the customer's documented business or prior activity require review before release, regardless of amount.

title: Employee or Insider Activity
clause: Transactions involving institution employees or insiders that deviate from expected personal activity require independent review to mitigate insider abuse and conflicts of interest.

title: Charity and NPO Risk
clause: Nonprofit and charitable accounts conducting cross-border transfers to higher-risk regions require enhanced review to mitigate diversion-of-funds risk, while avoiding undue disruption of legitimate activity.

title: Currency Exchange Aggregation
clause: Currency exchanges by the same person aggregating above the reporting threshold within a single business day must be aggregated and reported. Splitting exchanges to stay below the threshold is a structuring indicator.

title: Use of Multiple Accounts to Disperse Funds
clause: A single source of funds dispersed across multiple newly opened accounts shortly before onward transfer is a layering indicator and must be flagged even where individual transfers are unremarkable.

title: Inconsistent Occupation and Volume
clause: Transaction volumes materially inconsistent with a customer's stated occupation or business require review. Unexplained high-velocity activity on a low-expected-volume profile is a reportable indicator.

title: Sanctions Ownership Threshold
clause: An entity owned 50 percent or more, in aggregate, by one or more sanctioned parties is itself treated as sanctioned. Transactions with such entities must be blocked even if the entity is not separately listed.

title: Recordkeeping for Funds Transfers
clause: Records for funds transfers at or above the recordkeeping threshold must be retained with originator and beneficiary details. Transfers processed without retained records are a control failure and must be remediated.

title: Early Loan Repayment Anomaly
clause: Unexpected early repayment of a loan with funds inconsistent with the customer's profile, particularly shortly after disbursement, is a money-movement indicator and must be reviewed.

title: Concentration Account Misuse
clause: Use of internal concentration or omnibus accounts to obscure the identity of the underlying customer in a transaction is prohibited. Customer-identifying information must accompany movements through such accounts.

title: Refusal to Provide Information
clause: A customer's refusal to provide required identifying or source-of-funds information, or provision of information that cannot be verified, requires the transaction to be held and the relationship reviewed.

title: Routine Low-Value Activity Clearance
clause: Routine transactions that are low in value relative to the customer's established profile, consistent with expected activity, and below applicable reporting thresholds do not by themselves require reporting or escalation and may be cleared as compliant. Clearing on this basis requires that no reporting, structuring, sanctions, or due-diligence rule is triggered.

title: Verified Counterparty Clearance
clause: Transactions with a previously verified counterparty and a documented, consistent source of funds, absent any other risk indicator, are consistent with the customer's profile and may be cleared as compliant.

title: Single Below-Threshold Transaction Clearance
clause: A single, isolated cash transaction below the $10,000 reporting threshold that is consistent with the customer's established profile does not itself require a currency transaction report. This clearance applies ONLY to a one-off transaction and must NOT be used where there are multiple or repeated sub-threshold transactions across days, branches, or accounts; such patterns are structuring and must be flagged, not cleared.

title: Salary and Benefit Payment Clearance
clause: Recurring salary, payroll, pension, or government benefit payments from an established and verified source that are consistent with the customer's documented profile are expected activity and may be cleared as compliant.

title: Own-Account Transfer Clearance
clause: Transfers between a customer's own verified accounts for documented cash-management or personal purposes, absent other risk indicators, may be cleared as compliant.

title: Watchlist Name-Match Screening
clause: All parties to a transaction must be screened against the institution's sanctions watchlist by name. An exact name match to a listed party requires the transaction to be blocked and flagged for escalation. A close but inexact name match is a potential hit and must be sent for human review rather than cleared, because a missed true match is a sanctions violation while a false match costs only review time.
